text: {"title":"Fiber-scale neighbor crowding","group_name":"fiber_collision_crowding_context","family":"spectroscopic_selection_geometry","summary":"Encode whether an object lies in a close angular crowding regime where SDSS fiber-placement and deblending constraints can make galaxies, blended stars, and compact quasars appear with different catalog-neighborhood signatures.","depends_on":[],"strategy":"Using only predictor rows and no labels, convert alpha and delta to spherical unit vectors with RA wrap handled by geometry, then compute nearest-neighbor context on the combined unlabeled catalog or, if unavailable, within the supplied frame. For each object, exclude itself and query angular neighbors at fixed SDSS-motivated scales: 2 arcsec for unresolved blends, 3 arcsec for legacy fiber aperture, 10 arcsec for close deblending/crowding, 55 arcsec for SDSS-I/II fiber collision, 62 arcsec for BOSS-like collision, and 120 arcsec as a local control annulus; these scales are motivated by SDSS documentation on star-galaxy classification and fiber placement (https://www.sdss4.org/dr17/algorithms/classify/, https://www.sdss3.org/dr10/help/glossary.php). Derive log1p neighbor counts inside each radius, nearest and second-nearest angular distances capped at 120 arcsec and represented as log distance ratios to 55 arcsec, signed margins to the 3, 55, and 62 arcsec thresholds, close-pair indicators, and companion-similarity summaries for neighbors inside 120 arcsec using median absolute differences in redshift, r magnitude, and adjacent colors. If an object has no neighbor within 120 arcsec, set counts to zero, capped distances to 120 arcsec, threshold margins to negative outside values, and similarity summaries to robust high sentinel values based on finite training-frame quantiles.","expected_signal":"Close angular companions are not uniformly distributed by class: galaxies cluster and are affected by close-pair spectroscopy, stars can be crowded or blended in dense fields, while most quasars are comparatively isolated except for special cases, so explicit fiber-scale crowding may clarify ambiguous STAR/GALAXY/QSO boundaries beyond raw sky coordinates.","risk":"This can be partly redundant with broader sky-environment features, may be unstable if the train/test catalog is only a sparse sample of the true sky footprint, and fixed SDSS angular thresholds could overfit survey-specific selection artifacts despite using no target labels."}
